expectations.text: Picks 'closer to business policy' as correct
expectations.evidence: Line 1: 'closer to business policy' is right; 'more generic / more reused' is the common misconception

expectations.text: Level = distance from I/O, not genericness
expectations.evidence: 'level = distance from input/output, not generality or reuse count'

expectations.text: Concrete examples: business class as high-level vs reused utility as low-level
expectations.evidence: PriceQuoteService high-level vs MoneyUtils low-level despite reuse

expectations.text: Protection direction: keep DB/UI/framework churn from climbing up
expectations.evidence: 'DB schema churn now climbs into pricing policy' red-flag cited

expectations.text: Cautions against conflating reuse-count with levelness
expectations.evidence: 'Reuse count is irrelevant to that question.'
